items.name: Browser Harness
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Browser Harness – Gives LLM freedom to complete any browser task
items.summary: Browser Harness — Minimal browser harness that exposes raw Chrome DevTools to LLMs.
items.why_included: Commenters treated it as a concrete browser-agent pattern worth trying, not just a wrapper demo.
items.evidence: HN compared it with Playwright, agent-browser, and chrome devtools MCP, and praised the SKILL.md-plus-helper approach.

items.name: Endless Toil
items.source_story_title: Hear your agent suffer through your code
items.summary: Endless Toil — Audio feedback layer that turns AI code smell into sounds.
items.why_included: The thread framed it as a real developer-tool product, not just a joke.
items.evidence: Commenters debated its heuristic scans, asked for a Cursor plugin, and argued sound could act as observability.

items.name: CC-Canary
items.source_story_title: CC-Canary: Detect early signs of regressions in Claude Code
items.summary: CC-Canary — Regression tracker for spotting Claude Code behavior drift.
items.why_included: It drew practical discussion about prompt and skill changes affecting Claude Code quality.
items.evidence: Commenters worried about self-measurement bias, debated drift, and shared tools for tracking Claude Code performance over time.

items.name: Spinel
items.summary: Spinel — Experimental Ruby AOT compiler that emits native code.
items.why_included: HN spent real time on its subset tradeoffs and what Ruby loses without metaprogramming.
items.evidence: Comments highlighted the no-eval/no-threads restrictions and noted Matz built it with Claude in about a month.

items.name: HX DOS Extender
items.summary: HX DOS Extender — DOS compatibility layer that helped SDL run on DOS.
items.why_included: It was cited as a practical predecessor for the new SDL-on-DOS work.
items.evidence: Commenters said HXDOS already made the setup possible and compared it with FreeDOS and other legacy environments.

items.name: Tiller
items.source_story_title: Could a Claude Code routine watch my finances?
items.summary: Tiller — Spreadsheet sync service for bank and credit-card transactions.
items.why_included: It was recommended as a boring, dependable alternative to using Claude for finance monitoring.
items.evidence: A commenter said Tiller works well for sheet-based categorization and cashflow math, with no hallucinations.

items.name: Lunch Money
items.source_story_title: Could a Claude Code routine watch my finances?
items.summary: Lunch Money — Personal finance app with Plaid-backed transaction tracking.
items.why_included: It surfaced as a real-world finance tool people already use alongside LLMs.
items.evidence: A commenter said they use Lunch Money for tracking and built an LLM-driven CLI around its API.

items.name: Plaid
items.source_story_title: Could a Claude Code routine watch my finances?
items.summary: Plaid — Financial-data platform that now has a self-serve path for hobbyists.
items.why_included: A Plaid rep stepped in to clarify non-business onboarding in the thread.
items.evidence: The comments explained the new free-trial flow, the business sales path, and the support tradeoffs between them.

items.name: FILCO
items.source_story_title: Diatec, known for its mechanical keyboard brand FILCO, has ceased operations
items.summary: FILCO — Durable mechanical keyboards remembered for premium build quality.
items.why_included: The closure thread turned into a long praise session for the brand’s keyboards.
items.evidence: Commenters called the boards bulletproof and daily-driver reliable, even while criticizing Diatec’s stagnation.
